              summarized in the M-300 report of the Interstate Commerce Commis­           dustries which posted increases in employment over the specified time
              sion and relate to all employees except executives, officials, and staff    span. The indexes are calculated from 172 unpublished seasonally ad­
              assistants (ICC group I) who received pay during the month. Gross           justed employment series (two-digit nonmanufacturing industries and
              average hourly earnings are computed by dividing total compensation         three-digit manufacturing industries) covering all nonagricultural
              by total hours paid for. Average weekly hours are obtained by               payroll employment in the private sector. A more detailed discussion

                     Labor turnover is the gross movement of wage and salary workers             From a sample composed of establishments reporting for both the
                  into and out of employed status with respect to individual                  previous and current months, the ratio of current month employment
                  establishments. This movement, which relates to a calendar month, is        to that of the previous month is computed. This is called a "link
                  divided into two broad types: Accessions (new hires and rehires) and        relative." The estimates of employment (all employees, including pro­
                  separations (terminations of employment initiated by either employer        duction and nonproduction workers together) for the current month
                  or employee). Each type of action is cumulated for a calendar month         are obtained by multiplying the estimates for the previous month by
                  and expressed as a rate per 100 employees. The data relate to all           these "link relatives." In addition, small bias correction factors are
